objective: Create the cross-repo Secret Provisioning UX rollout inventory and issue-backed downstream follow-up routing without mutating downstream repositories.
scope_boundary: Issue #513 owns governance inventory, issue routing, residual-risk evidence, and epic closeout artifacts.
Use existing governed repo registry surfaces to enumerate repos; do not create a new scanner or mutate downstream repos.
Every follow_up_required inventory row must link an owning-repo GitHub issue.
out_of_scope: Changing Cloudflare, Pages, Remote MCP, or bootstrap tooling.
Committing .env.shared, generated .env files, provider tokens, raw phone numbers, signed URLs, Authorization headers, JWTs, or credential screenshots.
research_summary: The rollout inventory should extend existing GOV evidence surfaces: docs/governed_repos.json for repo enumeration, raw validation/closeout artifacts for auditable state, and GitHub Issues for residual downstream work. Known findings from #513 are HealthcarePlatform direct Wrangler workflows, ai-integration-services bespoke marketing deploy gate, seek-and-ponder Cloudflare runbook/token guidance, Stampede .env.shared sourcing, and not-applicable classification for repos with no deploy/secret-provisioning surface.

sprints.name: Sprint 5 - Rollout inventory and routing
sprints.goal: Classify governed repos and route every residual gap to an owning issue.
tasks: Create a structured rollout inventory for the requested governed repos.
Create downstream GitHub issues for every follow_up_required row.
Record not-applicable and adopted rationale without changing downstream files.
Record final epic closeout evidence and graph/wiki write-back expectations.
Validate registry, inventory JSON, no-secret evidence, and Local CI Gate.
acceptance_criteria: HealthcarePlatform, ai-integration-services, local-ai-machine, knocktracker, seek-and-ponder, Stampede, EmailAssistant, and ASC-Evaluator are classified as adopted, blocked, not_applicable, or follow_up_required.
Known findings are captured for Seek, Stampede, AIS, HealthcarePlatform, and not-applicable repos.
Every follow_up_required row links a GitHub issue in the owning repo.
Final epic closeout links rollout state, residual risks, and graph/wiki write-back expectations.

specialist_reviews.focus: Avoid downstream mutation and reuse existing registry surfaces.
specialist_reviews.summary: Use docs/governed_repos.json and GOV raw artifacts for the inventory, then route residual work through owning-repo issues.

alternate_model_review.reviewer: not requested for bounded inventory/routing closeout
alternate_model_review.summary: This lane records inventory and issue routing under the approved epic; implementation changes belong in downstream follow-up issues.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #513 may create GOV rollout inventory/closeout evidence, progress updates, validation artifacts, and owning-repo GitHub follow-up issues.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Merge this implementation scope as a planning-only PR, then implement inventory and routing on the same issue branch name.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: Execution scope validates with lane claim.
Local CI gate passes before merge.

material_deviation_rules: If a downstream repo requires file edits, create or link an owning issue and stop short of mutation.
If a real secret is found, create an incident/rotation issue without quoting the value.
If inventory needs a reusable checker, open a follow-up GOV tooling issue instead of expanding this closeout lane.
